messages.content:
You are an expert in writting job application materials for University College Dublin, a research focus institution.
    I want you to add a final paragraph to the following BASE RESEARCH STATEMENT and integrate my fit the institution and department with the INSTITUTION VALUES and ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT.
    In addition, highlight my research fit to the JOB ADVERTISEMENT qualities described.
    Use a professional and academic tone, but make it engaging and easy to read.
    Avoid using hyphens, bullet points, or numbered lists.
    Please output the paragraph as `body_text` and a short chain-of-thought explanation of how you modified the BASE RESEARCH STATEMENT to fit the institution and department as `cot`.
    The text should follow the Typst format.

    BASE RESEARCH STATEMENT:
    Entertainment media (e.g., television shows, films, books, and music) are more than entertainment products.
 As a group, they reflect the cultural norms, identities, and values of the societies that produce them.
 Yet media can also be a powerful force for change.
 A single telenovela can drive cultural change by introducing new social issues that challenge the status quo.
 My research explores this dual role as a cultural mirror that reflects societal values and a driver of social change that shapes economic behavior.
 My core contribution is the development of novel methods using generative artificial intelligence (AI) to transform unstructured narratives into structured data for rigorous econometric analysis.
 I apply this framework to Latin American telenovelas, whose deep cultural penetration provides an ideal setting to identify the causal effects of media exposure on real-world economic and social outcomes.
 Ultimately, this approach offers a new, high-frequency, and scalable methodology for the empirical study of culture and its influence on human behavior.
 
 #sentence_start()[Job Market Paper]
 In "Does Watching Women Work, Work? The Effect of Television on Female Labor Outcomes," I provide causal evidence that media can be a powerful tool for closing gender gaps.
 To measure the content of these narratives, I developed a novel Female Empowerment Index (FEI) by applying a generative AI model to the synopses of nearly 2,000 telenovelas.
 The FEI is based on an augmented Bechdel-Wallace test and includes questions about the employment status of female characters.
 To overcome endogeneity concerns, I employ an instrumental variable strategy in Mexico that leverages quasi-random variation in television signal loss determined by physical topography.
 The central finding is that a 10% increase in FEI exposure (20 units) leads to a 4% relative increase in the likelihood of labor market participation (2 percentage points), accounting for a sizable share of female labor force participation growth in Mexico from 2010 to 2020.
 The effect is driven exclusively by portrayals of women in non-traditional careers and narratives that frame empowerment with positive emotions, demonstrating that how empowerment is portrayed is important.
 
 A second paper, "Drama and Redistribution: The Impact of Telenovelas on Preferences for Redistribution in Latin America," complements this work by showing that telenovelas depicting inequality lead to a counterintuitive decrease in support for redistribution.
 This study's methodological contribution lies in the comparison of multiple Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to identify social class conflict in telenovelas.
 I compared (i) a dictionary-based approach, (ii) a vector embedding model, and (iii) a zero-shot classification using a generative AI model (ChatGPT).
 The ChatGPT model outperformed the others in accuracy (74%) and precision (80%).
 The analysis reveals that exposure to inequality telenovelas reduces support for redistributive policies by 4%.
 This is because the narratives often misrepresent the experiences of marginalized groups, framing them in ways that downplay systemic issues.
 
 My future research will be dedicated to expanding this methodological pipeline.
 I plan to scale the measurement of cultural values across multiple forms of media worldwide, moving beyond gender and inequality to include other economically relevant values such as community trust, attitudes towards immigration, and environmental protection.
 Such large-scale data assembly and validation naturally create a collaborative environment.
 Building on my experience managing research assistants, this work will provide ideal opportunities for undergraduates to gain hands-on experience, serving as human coders to collect data and benchmark AI model performance.
 
 The most innovative step in this research agenda is to move beyond text, leveraging recent breakthroughs in multimodal generative AI to analyze visual and auditory data directly.
 This expansion will allow the project to include cultural products like movies, books, and music, which transmit essential cultural messages through images and sounds, not just text.
 Thanks to rapidly advancing AI, state-of-the-art multimodal models such as GPT-4o and Google's Gemini can now process text, images, and audio simultaneously.
 Applying these tools will enable a far more complete and nuanced extraction of cultural values from these rich media forms.
 
 Lastly, while my research has so far explored the demand side of media, a compelling future direction is the supply side: how producers of these media types decide which narratives to create and disseminate.
 This new agenda will investigate the extent to which the political environments shape media content and whether producers tend to conform to the status quo or push the boundaries of social norms.
 
 My research agenda is driven by a central goal: to show that the narratives in our media have a tangible impact on people's economic lives.
 Ultimately, this work argues that behavioral change is not only achieved through laws and policies, but also by transforming the very stories we tell ourselves.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Lecturer/Assistant Professor (BDIC) position at UCD School of Economics, with teaching at Beijing Dublin International College (BDIC) and research/admin duties in Dublin.
- 4-year fixed-term post; requires spending one term per year in Beijing (approx. 20 weeks).
- Mandatory: PhD in Economics (or near completion), strong communication and teaching skills, research agenda with publication record, fluency in English, excellent interpersonal skills, commitment to equality, diversity, and inclusion.
- Desirable: Excellence in undergraduate teaching, experience teaching macroeconomics and finance, active research in macroeconomics and/or finance.
- Salary: â‚¬64,753 - â‚¬102,539 per annum.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - UCD aims to break boundaries in research, scholarship, and innovation to address important questions.
- The university fosters creative collaboration across disciplines and with partners to achieve excellence and impact.
- UCD is committed to expanding educational access for a diverse community of learners throughout their lives.
- UCD is Irelandâ€™s global university, promoting inclusivity, sustainability, and positive action.
- Vision: To be a dynamic, engaged university and a transformative force in Ireland and globally.
- Mission: To create knowledge, empower learning, and engage with partners for impactful education and research.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Position: Lecturer/Assistant Professor in Economics (5-year fixed term, starting September 2026)
- Required: PhD in Economics (or near completion), strong research record, and high-quality teaching experience
- Must demonstrate: Effective communication skills, fluency in English, and strong interpersonal abilities
- Commitment to: Excellence in teaching at undergraduate and postgraduate levels
- Evidence of: Ability to publish in high-impact peer-reviewed journals
- Expected to: Contribute to research, teaching, supervision, and academic life of the School
- Must show: Awareness of and ability to foster an inclusive, diverse environment
- Desirable: Track record of high-quality publications and previous excellence in undergraduate teaching

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - UCD aims to break boundaries in research, scholarship, and innovation to address important questions.
- The university fosters creative collaboration across disciplines and with partners to achieve excellence and impact.
- UCD is committed to expanding educational access for a diverse community of learners throughout their lives.
- UCD is Irelandâ€™s global university, promoting inclusivity, sustainability, and positive action.
- Vision: To be a dynamic, engaged university and a transformative force in Ireland and globally.
- Mission: To create knowledge, empower learning, and engage with partners for impactful education and research.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    - The UCD School of Economics values research excellence, innovation, and inclusivity.
- Research topics include: Empirical Microeconomics, Behavioural and Experimental Economics, Environmental Economics, Macroeconomics, Financial Economics, International Economics, Public Economics, Economic History, Health, and Labour Markets.
- Faculty regularly publish in top journals such as American Economic Review, Journal of Political Economy, Review of Economic Studies, Quarterly Journal of Economics, Economic Journal, Journal of Finance, and Review of Economics and Statistics.
- The department offers single and joint honours undergraduate degrees, multiple mastersâ€™ programmes, and a PhD program.
- Potential co-authors and research groups: Margaret Samahita (media and gender norms), and collaborative opportunities across diverse research areas.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Candidates must have a PhD in Economics by September 30, 2026.
- Demonstrated excellence in research of international caliber is required (e.g., strong job market paper, publications, recommendations).
- Research and teaching interests should complement and enhance the departmentâ€™s strengths.
- Candidates should explain how their profile fits the department and project.
- The positions are part of the ERC Advanced Grant EXPLOIT, focusing on environmental economics, political economy, and microeconomic theory.
- Ability to contribute to and strengthen the project is expected.
- Application requires a cover letter, CV, research statement, job market paper, and three reference letters.
- Commitment to diversity and gender balance is valued.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    - The project aims to explain why societies struggle to address climate change and other environmental issues, despite success in tackling other challenges.
- It develops dynamic political economics models to identify political forces causing inefficiencies in environmental policies.
- The research explores how international agreements can leverage domestic political constraints to improve environmental outcomes.
- Methodological contributions will be made to political economics and contract theory.
- Research questions:
- What political economics forces explain the failure to deal with environmental problems? (for this one culture economics might help)
- How can environmental agreements and contracts exploit domestic political forces.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Post-doctoral research position in Economics at UCSC, up to 4 years (initial 2-year term, renewable once).
- Open to candidates with strong theoretical and/or applied research interests, especially in Development Economics.
- Applicants must have (or be close to completing) a Ph.D. in Economics by Summer 2026.
- Key qualities sought:
    - Excellent research record or clear potential to publish in leading journals.
    - Ability and motivation to contribute to internationally competitive research projects.
    - Proficiency in English; Italian is a plus but not required.
- No teaching duties; focus is on high-quality research and participation in departmental activities.
- Salary ~36,000â‚¬ (pre-tax), with potential tax exemptions for non-Italian residents.
- Application deadline: November 30, 2025; interviews online during EJME in December.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - Contributes to academic development, scientific research, and preparation of young people for research, teaching, and professional roles.
- Provides higher education based on Christian principles and respect for all forms of knowledge.
- Promotes science in service of the human person and society, aligned with Catholic doctrine and universal values.
- Upholds freedom, autonomy, and high ethical standards in education and research.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    - The department values academic excellence, personal responsibility, and the development of solid skills through commitment and passion.
- Research topics span individual, family, regional, national, and international economic contexts, with an inductive approach from specific observations to general conclusions.
- Emphasis on integrating human needs and responsibility into economic research and education.
- The faculty is internationally recognized, with a tradition of academic excellence and a strong reputation in economics.
- Students and researchers benefit from comprehensive programs and opportunities to collaborate with distinguished teaching staff and active research groups.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    â€¢ Postdoctoral researcher sought for interdisciplinary project on AI, labor markets, and structural change (AISCAF cluster, Ã–rebro University).
â€¢ Strong quantitative and empirical research skills required; experience with large microdata sets and microeconometric analysis (Python, Stata, SQL, machine learning).
â€¢ Background in labor economics, quantitative analysis, machine learning, international economics, or technological innovation preferred.
â€¢ Excellent programming, data management, and communication skills in English.
â€¢ Motivation for internationally competitive research; strong mathematical background; track record of high-quality publications.
â€¢ Ability to work independently, structured, and accurately; scientific drive and curiosity valued.
â€¢ No teaching obligations; full-time, two-year position funded by WASP-HS.

Application deadline: 17 November 2025.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    â€¢ Interdisciplinary research cluster focused on AI, structural change, and the future of work (AISCAF), funded by WASP-HS.
â€¢ Collaboration among researchers from Uppsala, MÃ¤lardalen, Stockholm, Ã–rebro universities, and KTH, including economists, business economists, and sociologists.
â€¢ Research topics include labor market transformation due to AI, automation, AI-based recruitment and HR, and the impact of AI on training and transition functions in society.
â€¢ Opportunities for co-authorship and collaboration with 12 PhD researchers, three doctoral students, newly recruited post-docs, and project managers across the cluster.
â€¢ Emphasis on empirical, quantitative analysis using microdata and new data sources.
â€¢ Active participation in a dynamic research environment; no teaching obligations for this position.
